content:
Key Findings

  • South Ribble Borough Council recorded £178.0M in total spending across 18,517 transactions from 2021/22 to 2025/26
  • The council's payment types are categorised as Unknown for the entire £178.0M
  • Every penny of the £178.0M was processed through methods classified as Unknown

Understanding Payment Types

South Ribble Borough Council's spending data reveals that all £178.0M was processed through payment types classified as Unknown. This lack of categorisation makes it challenging to analyse how the council distributes its funds across different types of payments. The absence of specific categories such as invoices, grants, or salaries obscures the financial landscape of the council's operations.

The £178.0M figure represents the total expenditure over five financial years, from 2021/22 to 2025/26. This period includes a wide range of services and activities that the council is responsible for, from waste management to social care. However, without detailed payment types, it is difficult to determine how these funds are allocated across different departments and services.

Payment Methods: A Blank Slate

The data shows that the payment methods for the entire £178.0M are also classified as Unknown. This means that the council has not provided any information on whether these payments were made via bank transfer, cheque, or other methods. The lack of transparency in payment methods can make it harder for residents to understand how their council tax contributions are being used.

Transparency in payment methods is crucial for accountability. Knowing whether payments are made electronically or via traditional methods can provide insights into the council's efficiency and modernisation efforts. For instance, electronic payments are generally faster and more secure, while cheques can be more prone to delays and fraud. The absence of this information leaves a significant gap in the public's understanding of the council's financial operations.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to South Ribble Borough Council about the specific payment types and methods used for their £178.0M expenditure
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to ask questions about the council's financial transparency
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see if there are any details about payment methods and types
  • Write to your local councillor about the lack of transparency in South Ribble Borough Council's payment types and methods
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and gain a deeper understanding of the council's financial activities
This article is based on South Ribble Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
